claim: The opportunity size for agentic AI is limited primarily by defect rate (frequency and severity of errors), not by frontier model capability advances
actionable: Prioritize defect reduction in agent systems over chasing latest model releases; reliability unlocks the broadest opportunity
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Marc Brooker: It's Time to Be Right

claim: Low-consequence errors are acceptable; high-consequence errors kill adoption; the ideal is low frequency of low-consequence errors (everybody can use it)
actionable: Design agents to fail gracefully with low-impact errors; use formal verification and policy enforcement to prevent high-consequence failures
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Marc Brooker: It's Time to Be Right

claim: Frameworks like Hydro (distributed protocol correctness in Rust) and Cedar (formally-reasoned policy language) make agents correct by construction
actionable: Use formal verification frameworks for mission-critical agents; Cedar for policy, Lean for proofs, Hydro for distributed systems
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Marc Brooker: It's Time to Be Right

claim: Auto-formalization converts natural language policies into mathematically precise specifications (Cedar, Lean), exposing internal inconsistencies during formalization
actionable: Use auto-formalization tools to translate human intent into formal specs; conversation during formalization catches ambiguities
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Marc Brooker: It's Time to Be Right

claim: Deterministic agent policy (pre/post conditions, formal gates) allows flexibility while maintaining guarantees; agent creativity constrained by mathematically precise rules
actionable: Encode agent constraints as formal Cedar policies with pre/post conditions; this balances agent flexibility with safety guarantees
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Marc Brooker: It's Time to Be Right

claim: Benchmarks should measure failure severity, not just failure density; 'pass at 10' hides critical errors that appear only in extreme cases
actionable: Design evals that classify failures by consequence; track both rate and severity, not just pass rates
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Marc Brooker: It's Time to Be Right
